note: Session Limit exposes a config settings object, an event-subscriber enforcement service, three events, and per-role limits, but defines no permissions or plugins. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) since they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want to stop a single user account from being logged in on more than a set number of devices at once. Which contrib module does this and what config object holds its settings?
evals.expected_output: The Session Limit module caps simultaneous active sessions per user; settings live in the session_limit.settings config object, configured at /admin/config/people/session-limit.

evals.prompt: Which Session Limit setting sets the site-wide default maximum number of concurrent sessions per user, what is its default value, and what value means unlimited?
evals.expected_output: The session_limit_max key on session_limit.settings (default 1) sets the default max sessions; 0 means unlimited.

evals.prompt: In Session Limit, which setting controls what happens when the limit is exceeded, and which value blocks the new login versus ending the oldest session versus asking the user?
evals.expected_output: session_limit_behaviour on session_limit.settings: 0 = ask the user which session to end (default), 1 = drop the oldest session(s), 2 = prevent/block the new session.

evals.prompt: How do I give a specific role a different (or unlimited) session limit in Session Limit, and how is the effective limit chosen when a user has several roles?
evals.expected_output: Per-role overrides are stored in session_limit_roles (role id -> integer limit, -1 = unlimited); a user gets the largest applicable role limit, or unlimited if any role is -1, otherwise the default session_limit_max.

evals.prompt: In Session Limit, which service enforces the limit, what event does it dispatch when a user has too many sessions, and on which kernel event does it run?
evals.expected_output: The session_limit service (Drupal\session_limit\Services\SessionLimit) subscribes to KernelEvents::REQUEST (priority 32) and dispatches the session_limit.collision event when active sessions exceed the max.

evals.prompt: I want to programmatically skip the Session Limit check for certain requests in a custom Drupal module. Which event do I subscribe to and which method do I call?
evals.expected_output: Subscribe to the session_limit.bypass event (SessionLimitBypassEvent) and call $event->setBypass(TRUE) to skip the check for that request.

evals.prompt: In Session Limit, how can custom code prevent a particular session from being ended, or change the logout message a user sees? Which event and methods?
evals.expected_output: Subscribe to session_limit.disconnect (SessionLimitDisconnectEvent); call preventDisconnect(TRUE) to keep the session, or setMessage() to change the logout message.

evals.prompt: Which permission gates the Session Limit settings form, and how do I make the limit also apply to the User 1 super-admin account?
evals.expected_output: The settings form (route session_limit.config_form) is gated by the core 'administer site configuration' permission (the module defines none of its own); enable session_limit_admin_inclusion to also limit User 1, who is otherwise never checked.
